questions.question: What is the 'One Problem Rule' for lead magnets?

options.text: Your lead magnet should solve every problem your ICP has.
Your lead magnet must solve ONE specific, urgent problem related to your paid offering.
You should only create one lead magnet ever.
Lead magnets should be as general as possible to appeal to more people.

questions.explanation: Specificity wins. A lead magnet that solves one urgent problem will convert better than a broad, generic resource because it provides immediate, actionable value.

questions.question: Which popup trigger typically has the highest conversion rate?

options.text: Immediate popup when page loads.
Exit intent popup (triggers when mouse moves toward close button).
Popup that appears every 10 seconds.
Popup that covers the entire screen with no close button.

questions.explanation: Exit intent popups convert at 5-10% because they catch visitors at the moment they're about to leave, offering one last chance to provide value without interrupting their reading experience.

questions.question: What is a 'Content Upgrade' and why does it convert better than generic lead magnets?

options.text: A more expensive version of your product.
A blog post-specific lead magnet that is directly related to the content the reader is already consuming, creating higher relevance and conversion.
An upgraded email template with better design.
A premium newsletter tier.

questions.explanation: Content upgrades work because they're hyper-relevant. If someone is reading about cold email, offering them 'Cold Email Templates' is far more compelling than a generic 'Marketing Guide.'

questions.question: What are the 6 essential elements of a high-converting landing page?

options.text: Logo, navigation menu, sidebar, footer, ads, and a form.
Clear headline, subheadline, benefits bullets, social proof, minimal form, and strong CTA.
Video, testimonials, pricing table, FAQ, blog links, and contact info.
Header image, company history, team photos, awards, press mentions, and a phone number.

questions.explanation: A landing page should have singular focus. These 6 elements work together to communicate value, build trust, and make it easy to convert without distractions.

questions.question: What is the difference between a TOFU and MOFU lead magnet?

options.text: TOFU is for beginners; MOFU is for experts.
TOFU (Top of Funnel) addresses broad problems for large audiences; MOFU (Middle of Funnel) addresses specific problems for qualified, closer-to-purchase audiences.
TOFU is free; MOFU costs money.
There is no difference; they are the same thing.

questions.explanation: TOFU lead magnets cast a wide net (e.g., 'SEO Checklist'), while MOFU lead magnets target people further along the buying journey with more specific needs (e.g., 'SaaS Pricing Calculator').

questions.question: Complete the practice exercise: Identify the ONE specific, urgent problem your ICP faces that you can solve in 10 minutes or less. Choose a format (PDF, checklist, template, mini-course, tool) and write the headline and 3 benefit bullets for your landing page. Why would your ICP trade their email for this?
promptHints: Focus on a problem that causes daily frustration.
Make the benefit immediate and actionable.
Consider what you wish someone had given you when you faced this problem.
